what_it_does: Acceptance test for Suite._chat_context that verifies the RHM context includes live whole-interface state (chat/embed models, modes, verbs, inbox, graphs) and degrades legibly when the embed endpoint is unavailable.
observations: Bootstraps a temporary FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite against the nodes/ directory.
Stubs suite._models_cache and suite.models_at so assertions target registry values, not network availability.
Creates a graph with constant and uppercase nodes and surfaces two generative review items.
Checks that _chat_context contains chat models, embed models, modes, current mode, RHM verbs, a verb gloss, inbox count, item titles, and the graph name.
Includes a length budget check requiring the context stay under 16000 characters.
Simulates an unreachable embed endpoint and asserts the context still builds with a degraded marker while retaining chat models.
standouts: Explicit 16k context budget check with a rationale in a comment.
Fail-loud-legible degradation test for a downstream endpoint failure.
Self-referential grounding check: the context must list the RHM's own verbs and what they do.
conventions: Uses a hand-rolled check() helper with a global PASS counter instead of a test framework.
Cleans up the temporary store directory in a try/finally block.
Heavy inline comments explaining the intent of each assertion.
concerns: Relies on substring matching, which can be brittle if context formatting changes.
Tests a private method (_chat_context) directly, coupling the test to internal implementation.
Global PASS counter and check() helper are not thread-safe and lack structured reporting.
Hardcodes specific model names and verb names that may drift.
notes: An acceptance test that locks down what the RHM chat context must contain and how it should behave when the embed model endpoint is down.
questions: What does RHM stand for in this codebase?
What is the full set of presence modes and how is the current mode determined?
How does _chat_context format the verb gloss and where is the canonical verb list defined?
What does surface_review do with the origin and kind fields?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it verifies the enriched chat context carries live whole-interface values and degrades legibly.
apparent_themes: Live registry state over fabricated context.
intention_signals: To prevent the RHM from hallucinating interface state by ensuring its context is built from live registry values and to make partial endpoint failures visible rather than silent.
novelty: Testing that the assistant's own capability verbs and their glosses, plus pending inbox items, are explicitly present in the context.
contribution: A concrete acceptance contract for _chat_context contents and failure-mode behavior, useful as a regression guard when the context format or model endpoints change.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying Suite._chat_context includes live models, modes, verbs, inbox items, and graphs while staying under a 16k budget and degrading legibly if the embed endpoint is down.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce that the RHM's chat context is grounded in real registry state and remains useful even when optional endpoints fail.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments PASS, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: Hand-rolled test assertion helper used throughout the script; it aborts on failure and tallies passing checks in a global counter.

symbols.does: Raises RuntimeError to simulate an unreachable embed endpoint.
symbols.description: Standalone function assigned to suite.models_at during the degradation test so _chat_context can be exercised under a failing model-read path.
